claim: Adaptive replay improves sample efficiency in sparse-reward manipulation tasks.
evidence: Adaptive replay improves sample efficiency in sparse-reward manipulation tasks. Across five simulated robotics tasks, the method reached the target success threshold with 32% fewer environment interactions than the uniform replay baseline.

claim: Method X outperforms PPO on sparse-reward robotics tasks.
evidence: We compare Method X against PPO on sparse-reward robotics tasks. Method X performed comparably to PPO, with no statistically significant improvement in sample efficiency.

claim: Adaptive replay improves sample efficiency in all robotics tasks.
evidence: Adaptive replay improves sample efficiency in sparse-reward manipulation tasks. The effect was weaker in dense-reward locomotion tasks.

claim: Method X uses a transformer policy with offline pretraining.
evidence: We compare Method X against PPO on sparse-reward robotics tasks. The ablation study keeps the exploration module active in all variants.
